reasoning: Starlink secured its initial license approval in Chad in November 2024, at which time Elon Musk prematurely tweeted that the service was 'available.' The final, formal operating license was signed on March 13, 2025. Nearby Niger, which cleared similar regulatory hurdles around the exact same time, saw its consumer launch happen almost immediately. Although the Chadian government announced that the service would be available 'nationwide by the end of 2025', Starlink typically flips countries to 'Available' on its map as soon as it begins accepting commercial orders or a region becomes active, rather than waiting for full-scale nationwide penetration. Given that the major regulatory and licensing barriers are now resolved as of mid-March, Starlink has more than 3.5 months before the July 1, 2025 deadline to commence operations and update its map to 'Available.' The base rate for Starlink going live quickly after final operating license signatures is very high. Thus, a map update before July 1 is more likely than not, despite the 'end of 2025' timeline caveat referring to wider adoption.
